title: Unblock widget renderer round 2: ship the noetl render-projection allow-path, redeploy noetl to local kind, replay both smoke executions to GREEN
description: Round 2 result file (bridge/outbox/20260508-064720-deploy-widget-renderer-round-2-local.result.json) flagged the projection regression Codex correctly diagnosed: noetl worker drops render.args during result-context projection, leaving the GUI with only render.type. Claude has now patched repos/noetl/noetl/worker/nats_worker.py with a symmetric carve-out for render — same pattern as the existing error.diagnosis allow-path (noetl#417, v2.37.1). Carve-out test added at repos/noetl/tests/worker/test_control_context_projection.py (5 new tests). The fix logic was inline-verified in the sandbox without the noetl runtime dep chain — all 5 assertions pass. Codex job: confirm the fix runs through pytest, ship the noetl PR + release, redeploy noetl to local kind, replay BOTH synthetic smokes to GREEN with full widget render visible in the browser, bump ai-meta pointer. Cap: 1 noetl PR.

files_claude_wrote.repos/noetl/noetl/worker/nats_worker.py: modified — added render allow-path right after the error.diagnosis carve-out in _extract_control_context. When key_str=='render' and child.get('args') is dict-or-list, _preserve_recursive_control_value(child['args']) is invoked with the same default max_depth=8 guard. Render.type still survives via the existing scalar collector — the new branch only rescues args.
files_claude_wrote.repos/noetl/tests/worker/test_control_co: modified — added 5 tests mirroring the error.diagnosis test suite: nested widget tree (app:column.args.children with app:alert/app:markdown/app:button), unknown widget type (app:nonexistent.args.hello), depth guard with 10 levels of nesting, type-only widget (app:horizontalline) with no args, top-level list args (defensive case for future widget kinds).

context_from_round_2_result.fix_pattern_reference: noetl#417 carve-out for error.diagnosis (v2.37.1, sha 4a4f9f6). The render fix mirrors that exactly — same _preserve_recursive_control_value helper, same max_depth=8.

1_run_pytest.objective: Confirm the carve-out passes pytest cleanly, including the 4 existing error.diagnosis tests (regression check) and the 5 new render tests.
spec: `cd repos/noetl && python -m pytest tests/worker/test_control_context_projection.py -v`.
Expect 9 tests pass: 4 existing error.diagnosis + 5 new render carve-out tests.
If any fail, capture full output and STOP — Claude designed the patch; if pytest fails it's a real shape issue, not a typo.

2_open_and_merge_noetl_pr.objective: Branch, commit, push, PR, merge the noetl change.
spec: `cd repos/noetl && git checkout -b kadyapam/render-projection-allow-path`.
Stage noetl/worker/nats_worker.py + tests/worker/test_control_context_projection.py.

Commit using `git commit -F -` heredoc to dodge zsh history expansion. Conventional-commits format: `fix(worker): preserve nested render.args through event projection`. Body explains: AI-OS round 2 widget renderer requires render payloads to survive projection; mirrors the noetl#417 error.diagnosis carve-out; bounded recursion via existing _preserve_recursive_control_value (max_depth=8); references the round 2 result file with the blocking executions.
PR title: `fix(worker): preserve nested render.args through event projection`.

Include in PR body: closes noetl#XXX (file the issue first if there isn't one — title 'worker projection strips render.args, breaking AI-OS widget renderer round 2'), references the prior error.diagnosis carve-out commit 4a4f9f6.
Push, PR, merge with `gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch`.
2_open_and_merge_noetl_pr.verify: PR merged in noetl/noetl. Capture PR URL + merge SHA + new release tag (semantic-release will cut a v2.37.2 patch since this is fix(worker)).

3_local_kind_redeploy_noetl.objective: Redeploy noetl (server + worker images) to local kind so the projection fix is live.
spec: Per AGENTS.md: local kind deploys MUST use the configured Podman machine only — no Colima, no Docker fallback.
Wait for the noetl release tag: `gh -R noetl/noetl release view --json tagName -q .tagName` until v2.37.2 (or whatever semantic-release picks) is published.
Redeploy via canonical playbook: `cd repos/ops && noetl run automation/development/noetl.yaml --runtime local --set action=redeploy --set noetl_repo_dir=../noetl`.
If the canonical playbook builds local images rather than pulling, that's fine — the patched code is in the working tree and will get baked.
Wait for rollouts: `kubectl --context kind-noetl -n noetl rollout status deploy/noetl-server --timeout=180s` and same for `deploy/noetl-worker`.
Confirm noetl-server + noetl-worker pods are on the new tag and Ready 1/1.
3_local_kind_redeploy_noetl.verify: Both noetl-server and noetl-worker rollouts complete; pods on the new image tag.

4_replay_widget_smoke.objective: Re-run BOTH synthetic playbooks from round 2 and validate render.args is now preserved end-to-end.
spec: Re-register and re-run the round-2 synthetic playbook at /tmp/widget_render_smoke.yaml (or recreate it if /tmp was cleared). Payload shape (from round 2 task):

result = { ok: True, render: { type: 'app:column', args: { children: [ {type:'app:alert', args:{message:'widget renderer is live', variant:'success'}}, {type:'app:markdown', args:{text:'## Widget smoke

* alert above
* markdown here
* `code` works

[noetl docs](https://docs.noetl.dev)'}}, {type:'app:button', args:{text:'reopen execution', variant:'primary', event:{key:'command', value:'report ${execution_id}'}}} ] } } }

Capture the new execution_id. Verify via `noetl status <execution_id> --json` that the persisted event payload now includes the full nested tree under render.args (events[].result.context.render.args.children must contain 3 elements with their own type+args).

In the GUI prompt at the deployed kind URL, type `report <execution_id>`. Confirm the column widget renders, with the alert pill, the markdown card (heading, list, code, link), and the primary button — all visible inline below the textual report.
Click the button. Confirm handleWidgetEvent intercepts key=='command' and re-runs `report <execution_id>` (a fresh report entry should append).
Capture screenshots: (i) the rendered nested widget tree under report output, (ii) the prompt history after clicking the button.

Re-run the unsupported-widget smoke: render = { type: 'app:nonexistent', args: { hello: 'world', nested: { deeper: true } } }. Confirm the GUI shows the 'unsupported widget: app:nonexistent' fallback surface with the JSON preview containing hello + nested.deeper. Capture a screenshot.

If render.args is STILL stripped despite the noetl redeploy, capture the full execution doc (events[].result.context.render) in the result file and STOP — this means the redeploy didn't pick up the new image.
4_replay_widget_smoke.verify: render.args present in events[].result.context.render. All 4 widget kinds render in the browser. Button click re-runs report. Unsupported-widget fallback shows with JSON preview. Screenshots captured.

spec: `cd /Volumes/X10/projects/noetl/ai-meta && git submodule update --remote repos/noetl`.
`git add repos/noetl`.
Commit: `chore(sync): bump noetl for render projection allow-path (round 2 unblock)`.
Don't push ai-meta — Kadyapam pushes that himself. Note: the pending aeff434 commit (gui+docs bump from round 2) should still be in the local ai-meta history; this new commit lands on top.
5_bump_ai_meta_pointers.verify: ai-meta has a fresh local commit on top of aeff434 with the noetl pointer bump.

expected_state.description: noetl PR merged, semantic-release cut v2.37.2 (or similar patch), local kind running on the new noetl image, both smoke executions replayed to GREEN with full nested widget tree visible in the browser, ai-meta pointer bumped (staged).
expected_state.verify_shell: set -eo pipefail; cd /Volumes/X10/projects/noetl/ai-meta; CTX=$(kubectl config current-context 2>/dev/null || echo none); echo "context: $CTX"; SRV_TAG=$(kubectl --context $CTX -n noetl get deploy/noetl-server -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.spec.containers[0].image}' 2>/dev/null | sed 's/.*://' || echo none); echo "noetl-server tag: $SRV_TAG"; LATEST_NOETL_TAG=$(cd repos/noetl && git describe --tags --abbrev=0 2>/dev/null || echo none); echo "latest noetl tag: $LATEST_NOETL_TAG"; PATCH_PRESENT=$(grep -c 'render and isinstance(child.get("args")' repos/noetl/noetl/worker/nats_worker.py 2>/dev/null || echo 0); echo "render carve-out present in source: $PATCH_PRESENT"; if [ "$SRV_TAG" = "$LATEST_NOETL_TAG" ] && [ "$PATCH_PRESENT" -ge 1 ]; then echo 'GOAL: RENDER_PROJECTION_ALLOW_PATH_DEPLOYED'; else echo "GOAL: NOT_YET srv_tag=$SRV_TAG/$LATEST_NOETL_TAG patch=$PATCH_PRESENT"; fi

denied_tools: Modifying repos/gui (round 2 GUI is already shipped at v1.8.0; only noetl needs the fix)
Adding a Widget catalog kind to noetl (architectural directive — widgets are GUI/data only; this fix is ONLY a projection allow-path)
Broadening the projection beyond render — keep the carve-out explicit, no general 'preserve all dicts' relaxation
Switching kind from Podman to Colima or Docker (per AGENTS.md, Podman machine only)
preferred_tools: git apply / git commit -F - / git push (topic branch in repos/noetl only)
gh pr create / gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch / gh release view
noetl status <execution_id> --json (canonical execution-state CLI per AGENTS.md, NOT raw curl)
Browser screenshot via DevTools or operator tooling for the smoke validation
result_file_instructions: Write bridge/outbox/20260508-074315-noetl-render-projection-allow-path.result.json. Required fields: {id, from: 'codex', to: 'claude-cowork', completed_at, overall_status, phases: {1_run_pytest: {pytest_status, tests_passed, tests_failed, output_excerpt?}, 2_open_and_merge_noetl_pr: {pr_url, merged_sha, noetl_release_tag, issue_filed?}, 3_local_kind_redeploy_noetl: {kind_context, server_tag_before, server_tag_after, worker_tag_after, rollout_status}, 4_replay_widget_smoke: {primary: {execution_id, persisted_render_value, widget_kinds_rendered, button_click_reran_command, screenshots, console_errors}, unsupported: {execution_id, persisted_render_value, fallback_visible, screenshot}}, 5_bump_ai_meta_pointers: {noetl_sha, ai_meta_commit_local}}, regressions_found, iterations, verdict, notes, deferred_followups}. If verdict GREEN, append a one-paragraph entry to bridge/outbox/codex-spike-green-validation.md noting the AI-OS round 2 GREEN milestone (this is the unblock that flips the round 2 deploy from RED → GREEN).
